title: Sautéed Apples and Onions
description: Sweet-savory sautéed apples and caramelized onions with cinnamon and thyme. A natural pairing for pork chops, but works alongside roasted chicken or pork tenderloin too.

ingredients.name: apples (Granny Smith or Honeycrisp), cored and thinly sliced
fresh thyme or parsley for garnish (optional)

instructions.text: Heat butter and olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat until butter is melted and bubbly.
Add sliced onions and cook 5–7 minutes, stirring occasionally, until softened and starting to caramelize.

Stir in apple slices, spreading evenly. Season with salt, pepper, cinnamon, and thyme if using. Cook another 5–7 minutes, stirring occasionally, until apples are softened but still hold their shape and onions are golden.
Optional: add a splash of apple cider vinegar and stir to deglaze, lifting any browned bits. Cook 1–2 minutes to reduce slightly.
Remove from heat. Garnish with fresh thyme or parsley if desired. Serve warm over or alongside pork chops.

notes:
Granny Smith adds tartness that cuts through pork fat; Honeycrisp is sweeter and holds its shape well. Either works.

The apple cider vinegar deglaze is optional but adds a nice brightness that balances the sweetness.
